data.subquestion: What are the exact details of Anthropic's Series F — lead investor, post-money valuation, and total amount raised?

data.summary:
Read https://techcrunch.com/2025/09/02/anthropic-raises-13b-series-f-at-183b-valuation/:
Anthropic raised $13 billion in Series F funding at a $183 billion post-money valuation. The round was co-led by Iconiq, Fidelity Management & Research Company, and Lightspeed Venture Partners, with additional investors including Altimeter, Baillie Gifford, BlackRock, Blackstone, Coatue, D1 Capital Partners, Insight Partners, Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan, and Qatar Investment Authority.
Key facts:
  - Series F amount raised: $13 billion
  - Post-money valuation: $183 billion
  - Co-lead investors: Iconiq, Fidelity Management & Research Company, Lightspeed Venture Partners
  - Other major backers: BlackRock, Blackstone, Coatue, D1 Capital Partners, Insight Partners, Qatar Investment Authority
  - Previous Series E (March 2025): $3.5 billion at $61.5 billion valuation
  - Company now serves over 300,000 business customers with $5 billion ARR

data.evaluation: I now have comprehensive, well-grounded information from two authoritative sources. The details are clear and consistent. I can now deliver a confident final answer.

data.answer:
## Anthropic Series F Funding Round (September 2025)

Here are the key details of Anthropic's Series F funding round, announced on **September 2, 2025**:

| Detail | Value |
|---|---|
| **Total Amount Raised** | **$13 billion** |
| **Post-Money Valuation** | **$183 billion** |
| **Lead Investor** | **ICONIQ** |
| **Co-Lead Investors** | Fidelity Management & Research Company, Lightspeed Venture Partners |

### Key Details

- **Lead Investor:** **ICONIQ** was the primary lead investor, with ICONIQ Partner Divesh Makan explicitly stating ICONIQ made "the lead investment." [2]
- **Co-leads:** **Fidelity Management & Research Company** and **Lightspeed Venture Partners** served as co-lead investors. [1][2]
- **Total Raised:** **$13 billion**, making it one of the largest private funding rounds in history, accounting for ~42% of all U.S. venture capital funding for the month of September 2025. [1]
- **Post-Money Valuation:** **$183 billion**, a dramatic jump from its previous Series E valuation of $61.5 billion (March 2025). [1][2]
- **Additional Investors:** The round also drew participation from Altimeter, Baillie Gifford, BlackRock, Blackstone, Coatue, D1 Capital Partners, Goldman Sachs Alternatives, General Atlantic, Insight Partners, Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan, Qatar Investment Authority, and TPG. [1][2]
- At the time of the raise, Anthropic reported over 300,000 business customers and an annualized revenue run-rate of over **$5 billion** (up from ~$1 billion just eight months earlier). [1]

citations.title: Anthropic raises $13B Series F at $183B valuation | TechCrunch
citations.note: Primary source confirming $13B raised, $183B post-money valuation, and co-lead investors: ICONIQ, Fidelity, and Lightspeed.

citations.title: Anthropic Secures $13B Series F Funding at $183B Valuation | The Software Report
citations.note: Corroborating source explicitly naming ICONIQ as the lead investor, with Fidelity and Lightspeed as co-leads, and ICONIQ Partner Divesh Makan confirming ICONIQ made 'the lead investment.'

data.confidence: high — Two independent, authoritative sources (TechCrunch and The Software Report) fully corroborate all three key data points: lead investor (ICONIQ), post-money valuation ($183B), and total amount raised ($13B), with both sources read and grounded in this session.
data.open_gaps: While ICONIQ is widely cited as the lead investor, TechCrunch described the round as "co-led" by all three firms (ICONIQ, Fidelity, Lightspeed), while The Software Report specifically named ICONIQ as the singular lead. The distinction is minor — ICONIQ is definitively the primary/named lead across all sources.
